Saturday preview: Can Panova return to her best and claim the  Oaks? (Saturday preview: Can Panova return to her best and claim the Oaks?)

Twin Hills Stud has released the 2026 fees for the resident trio of Daumier, Smart Missile, and Peltzer, with an emphasis on value that promises performance ringing true with the fees for each stallion.

Magic Millions have revealed the highly anticipated 2026 Magic Millions National Broodmare Sale catalogue, featuring 374 lots. Among the race filly highlights are Group 1 winners Captured By Love, Legarto, Quintessa, and Vibrant Sun, while the likes of Startantes and Abbey Marie in foal to commercial sires add appeal on day 2.

With 16 stallions across two states, Darley Australia have held the fees steady for their big guns of Too Darn Hot at $275,000, Anamoe at $110,000 and Street Boss at $66,000. Ghaiyyath returns in 2026 with a fee leap to $88,000 with the same fee for Harry Angel.

The G1 Robert Sangster Stakes on Saturday will be the battle of the broodmare sales with six of the field of 17 headed to either the Inglis Chairman’s Sale or the Magic Millions National Broodmare Sale in the coming months.

Falls across all metrics at the Goffs Breeze Up Sale were the story of the day as the tried horse market reacted to European economic conditions. The sale topper was a Mehmas colt who made £880,000, while Henry Dwyer was active with a pair of purchases.

Arrowfield Stud have released their 2026 service fees, and The Autumn Sun has stepped up to occupy the lead spot previously held by Snitzel and their father Redoute's Choice, standing for a career high fee of $137,500 (inc GST).

Superbly bred Loera is a candidate for the Listed Denise’s Joy Stakes after a strong maiden win, while stakes placed Marsept smashed her rivals at Geelong. Juvenile winners for Extreme Choice and All Too Hard both show plenty of upside.

Little Avondale Stud’s 2026 roster will be headed up by proven Group 1 sire Per Incanto, whose fee leaps to NZ$75,000 plus GST. His G1 Blue Diamond Stakes winning son Little Brose will stand his second season at NZ$15,000 plus GST.
